# Web Page Classifier — Project Overview

**Date:** March 28, 2026
**Author:** Generated by Claude, edited throughout project

---

## Problem

There is a file `raw_links.txt` — thousands of saved links accumulated over years. Articles from Habr, GitHub repositories, YouTube, stores, blogs. Everything is mixed together, nothing is sorted. Finding something specific is impossible.

Task: **automatically sort all links by categories**.

---

## Architecture — Data Enrichment Pipeline (ETL)

The project is organized as a **data enrichment pipeline** — sequence of Extract → Transform → Load steps. Each step is idempotent and can be run separately.

### Extract + Load
**Step 1** (`step1.py`) — **Load** phase. Takes `raw_links.txt`, extracts URLs with regex, deduplicates, puts into SQLite (`urls.db`) with `pending` status. Writes without processing.

### Extract + Transform
**Step 2** (`step2.py`) — **Extract + Transform** phases. Goes through each URL, makes HTTP request, extracts `<title>` and `og:description` from page. Enriches DB record with structured metadata. Works politely: random delays between requests, User-Agent rotation, retry with backoff. Parallelizes by domain (doesn't hammer one site from different threads simultaneously). Status → `done` or `error`.

### Transform (Annotation)
**Step 3** (`step3.py`) — **Transform** phase (annotation). Takes URL with title and description, sends to local LLM via Ollama. Model reads title and selects exactly one category from 31 fixed taxonomy (`config/taxonomy.py`). Domain rules narrow selection to section (e.g., `habr.com` → only IT & development). Result written to `urls.category` and `urls.tagged_by`.

### Orchestration
Everything managed via `main.py` with flags (idempotent, re-run skips processed):
```
python main.py                        # full ETL pipeline (step1 + 2 + 3)
python main.py --only-parse           # only Extract (step2)
python main.py --only-classify        # only Annotation (step3)
python main.py --re-tag --model X     # re-classify with different model
python main.py --stats                # DB statistics
```

**Key property:** each step is **idempotent** — re-running without flags doesn't touch already processed URLs.

---

## Development Timeline

### Sessions 1–3 (March 7) — Core

URL import, title parsing, LLM classification via Ollama, side-by-side model comparison. Main files: `step1.py`, `step2.py`, `step3.py`, `compare.py`, `db.py`, `main.py`.

### Sessions 4–5 (March 8) — Performance

Batching (batch of URLs in one LLM request), parallelism (`--workers`), benchmark to find optimal batch × workers parameters. `benchmark/benchmark.py`.

### Session 6 (March 8, evening) — Classification Quality

Switch from 1–3 tags to exactly 1 category. `--dry-run` for testing prompts without DB write. `--stats` for quick summary.

### Sessions 7–8 (March 9) — Configuration and Export

Settings extracted (`config/settings.py`) and prompts (`config/prompts.py`). XLSX export of model comparison. `temperature=0` for determinism. Structured HTTP error codes (404 vs 503 vs network).

### Session 9 (March 21) — Page Descriptions

Fill `og:description` for 5000+ URLs. Bypass JS anti-bot stubs. Three `--refetch-description` runs raised description coverage to 51%.

### Session 10 (March 25) — Taxonomy and Web UI

Fixed taxonomy: 31 categories in `config/taxonomy.py`. Domain rules (`config/domain_rules.py`) — github.com → "Programming" without LLM. Web UI on FastAPI (`web/`) for browsing and manual category editing. `manual_override` — protects manual tags from LLM overwrite.

### Session 11 (March 27) — Cleanup

Removed junk URLs (404, anti-bot, google.com). Removed old tag system — taxonomy is now sole source of categories. `deferred` status for deferred errors. Improved LLM answer parser.

### Session 12 (March 28) — ML Classifier

Dataset export (`export_dataset.py`), model training (`train_classifier.py`). Details below.

---

## DB State on March 28

- ~7000 URLs in database
- ~6600 with assigned category (LLM)
- 31 categories in taxonomy
- Top: "Artificial Intelligence" (1071), "Programming" (756), "Project Management" (654)

---

## ML Classifier

### Why

LLM classification works, but:
- **Slow**: 6000 URLs = 3–4 hours via Ollama
- **Non-deterministic**: one URL can get different category on re-run
- **Requires GPU + Ollama**: can't run on weak machine

Idea: train small model (`xlm-roberta-base`, 280MB) on labels already assigned by LLM. Model classifies in milliseconds, without Ollama, deterministically.

### How It Works

**`export_dataset.py`** — export from DB to JSONL:

```json
{"text": "Why large business flees ChatGPT / Habr [SEP] description... [SEP] habr.com", "label": "Artificial Intelligence"}
```

Model input: title + description (up to 200 chars) + domain. Domain matters — `github.com` = likely code, `habr.com` = tech blog.

**`train_classifier.py`** — fine-tune `xlm-roberta-base`:
- Splits data 70/15/15 (train/val/test), stratified
- Weighted loss — rare classes get higher weight
- Label smoothing 0.1
- Training ~6 min on RTX A4000 (16GB VRAM)
- Saves model, metrics, confusion matrix to `models/url_classifier/`

### Baseline Results (v1)

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Accuracy | 0.57 |
| Macro-F1 | **0.46** (goal — 0.80) |
| Training | 5.8 min |
| Classes | 31 |
| Train / Val / Test | 4643 / 996 / 996 |

Top classes (F1 > 0.60):
- Project Management: 0.72
- Equipment: 0.67
- Artificial Intelligence: 0.67
- Application Development: 0.67
- Cybersecurity: 0.65

Problematic (F1 < 0.30):
- Health and sports: 0.00 (2 test examples)
- Politics and law: 0.00 (3 examples)
- Food and beverages: 0.22 (3 examples)

### Why 0.46, Not 0.80

1. **Noisy labels**. Categories assigned by LLM, not human-verified. Model learns from teacher's mistakes.
2. **Class imbalance**. "Artificial Intelligence" — 1071 examples, "Books" — 9. Model rarely sees rare classes.
3. **31 classes is many**. Some overlap ("Literature" vs "Books", "Automation" vs "DevOps").

---

## What's Next

1. **Manual validation** of labels via Web UI — strongest lever for quality
2. **Merge rare categories** (31 → 20–25)
3. **`step4.py`** — inference with trained model in pipeline: instant classification of new URLs, fallback to LLM on low confidence
4. **Active learning** — show examples with lowest model confidence for priority manual review

Full ML development plan: `docs/ml-plan.md`

---

## File Structure

```
main.py                     — entry point, CLI
step1.py                    — URL import from file
step2.py                    — title/description parsing
step3.py                    — LLM classification (Ollama)
compare.py                  — model comparison
db.py                       — SQLite: schema, CRUD
export_dataset.py           — export to JSONL for training
train_classifier.py         — fine-tune xlm-roberta-base
config/
  settings.py               — all constants
  prompts.py                — LLM prompt templates
  taxonomy.py               — 31 categories (single source)
  domain_rules.py           — rules by domain
web/                        — FastAPI Web UI
benchmark/                  — batch × workers benchmark
docs/
  backlog.md                — log of all features (79+)
  ml-plan.md                — ML development plan
models/                     — trained models (in .gitignore)
```

## Manual Validation: How Much

Model learns from labels. If labels have 30% errors — model ceiling ~0.70 F1, no matter the architecture. Currently macro-F1 = 0.46, suggesting high noise.

**Minimum per class — 50 clean examples.** Empirical rule for fine-tune: less — model doesn't catch pattern.

### Breakdown by Priority

| Group | Classes | Total examples | Need to check | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Rare (< 50) | Books, Health, Business, Food, Politics, Testing, Music | ~160 | **all 160** | Each error critical, small sample |
| Medium (50–150) | Data, OS, Web, Vehicles, Media, Networks, Cybersec etc. | ~900 | **~450** (50%) | Random spot checks sufficient |
| Large (> 150) | AI, Programming, Management, Equipment, History | ~3570 | **~400** (50–100 per class) | Even 10% check boosts quality |

**Total: ~1000 URLs** for noticeable effect.

### Iterative Approach

No need for everything at once. Better in rounds:

1. **Round 1 (~300 URLs)** — check all rare classes + 20 random from large. Retrain. See delta.
2. **Round 2 (~300 URLs)** — focus on classes still with low F1. Retrain.
3. **Round 3** — if needed.

After each round: `export_dataset.py` -> `train_classifier.py` -> check metrics. Might be enough with 500 validations if labels turn out cleaner.

### Speed-up via Active Learning

Instead of random samples — show URLs where ML model least confident (lowest softmax confidence). These examples most informative for training. One check of "hard" example worth 5–10 random checks. This is what `step4.py` + Web UI could do together.

---

## Hardware

- AMD Ryzen 5 5500 / 64GB DDR4
- NVIDIA RTX A4000 (16GB VRAM)
- Ollama for local LLM run (qwen, llama, mistral, etc.)
